refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall provide an integrated performance requirement derivation, recording, and management system that captures worksite mission context (site conditions, operational objectives, safety constraints), derives quantitative performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins), maintains a traceable baseline history with calibration/update records, and enables performance monitoring against derived targets with documented evidence of compliance.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks specificity on the mechanism for derivation, the scope of context capture, and the traceability/history management aspect. Refinement clarifies that the system must actively ingest worksite inputs, apply a derivation logic, and maintain auditable records—critical for safety-critical systems and stakeholder confidence (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) worksite mission context is captured and stored; (2) performance targets are derived and documented with clear traceability to input context; (3) baseline history and calibration updates are recorded with timestamps and change rationale; (4) performance monitoring reports link actual performance to derived targets; (5) all records are accessible to authorized stakeholders.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Capture and store worksite mission context (site conditions, operational objectives, safety constraints, equipment configuration).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive quantitative performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context using defined algorithms or decision logic.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and maintain a traceable baseline of performance targets with version control, timestamps, and change rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Manage calibration and update events, documenting the reason, date, and authorized personnel for each change.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Enable performance monitoring and reporting against derived targets with documented evidence of compliance or deviation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Management Service—a dedicated software service that ingests worksite context via API, applies performance derivation algorithms, maintains a versioned baseline database, and exposes performance targets and history via query/reporting interfaces.
DP-B: Distributed Performance Derivation—embed performance derivation logic within Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, with a shared baseline registry maintained by a lightweight coordinator service.

DP-C: Manual Performance Planning with Digital Record—Site Manager manually derives performance targets using a web-based form, system records and versions the targets, and provides read-only access to monitoring data.

DP-D: Hybrid Intelligent Derivation—integrate machine learning models trained on historical worksite data to auto-suggest performance targets; Site Manager reviews and approves; system maintains baseline and monitors.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Management Service—a dedicated software service that ingests worksite context via API, applies performance derivation algorithms, maintains a versioned baseline database, and exposes performance targets and history via query/reporting interfaces.

potential_sources: DP-A couples the performance derivation logic (algorithm) to the baseline storage mechanism (database), which is acceptable because both are tightly related to the same functional intent: deriving and managing performance targets.
DP-A decouples performance derivation from Vehicle and Teleoperation operational logic, reducing complexity in those modules and enabling independent evolution of performance management.
DP-A introduces a dependency on worksite context input (via Tasking or a dedicated context API), which is a necessary external coupling but manageable via well-defined interfaces.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules (good separation of concerns) and moderate coupling to external context sources (acceptable and necessary). No circular dependencies identified. Coupling is primarily functional (data flow) rather than structural.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is available and can be reliably captured at system initialization and during operation.
Assumption: Performance derivation algorithms are well-defined and can be implemented deterministically (or with documented uncertainty bounds).
Risk: If worksite context is incomplete or inconsistent, derived targets may be invalid; mitigation: implement validation rules and require Site Manager approval before baseline commitment.
Risk: Centralized service becomes a single point of failure for performance management; mitigation: implement redundancy and fallback to cached baseline if service is unavailable.
Risk: Performance derivation logic may require domain expertise (e.g., soil mechanics, equipment dynamics) that is difficult to encode; mitigation: design for human-in-the-loop review and approval.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend clarifying the interface contract for worksite context input (what fields, format, validation rules) and the algorithm specification for performance derivation (e.g., lookup tables, regression models, expert rules) in the next decomposition level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides enterprise-level services including performance requirement derivation, recording, and management. The Performance Management Service is a logical component within NGHE Solution that owns the derivation algorithms, baseline database, and performance monitoring logic.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (site conditions, operational objectives, equipment configuration) from Site Manager or planning system; used as input to performance derivation algorithms.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational telemetry (actual cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; outputs derived performance targets and baseline history for monitoring and reporting.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outputs performance-related safety alerts (e.g., if actual performance deviates significantly from derived targets, indicating potential equipment malfunction or unsafe condition) to Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality personnel to access performance baseline, history, and monitoring reports via web or mobile interface.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for enterprise-level performance management. The Performance Management Service is a core system service that integrates with Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. High containment because performance derivation and baseline management are system-level concerns, not specific to any single subsystem. Low diffusion risk because the service is logically cohesive and can be implemented as a dedicated microservice or module within the NGHE Solution architecture.

Vehicle module is responsible for equipment operation and telemetry collection, not performance requirement derivation. Assigning performance management to Vehicle would couple operational logic with strategic planning logic, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion risk: performance derivation logic would be scattered across Vehicle firmware/software, making it difficult to maintain, test, and evolve independently.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote operation and control, not performance requirement derivation. Similar to Vehicle, assigning performance management to Teleoperation would create inappropriate coupling. High diffusion risk: performance derivation would be embedded in tele-operation control logic, making it difficult to reuse across different operational modes or equipment types.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring and alerts. Performance management has a safety dimension (e.g., deriving safety margins, alerting on performance deviations that indicate unsafe conditions), but the primary function is performance optimization and traceability, not safety assurance. Moderate containment because performance targets inform safety constraints, but performance derivation is not a core safety function. Moderate diffusion risk: if performance management is split between NGHE Solution and Safety Services, traceability and consistency may suffer.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (System Engineering or Performance Management team)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Performance requirement derivation, recording, and management is a system-level concern that must be owned by the NGHE Solution module. The Performance Management Service is a logical component within NGHE Solution that integrates with Vehicle (telemetry input), Teleoperation (operational context), and Safety Services (safety-related alerts). This assignment ensures clear ownership, reduces diffusion, and enables independent evolution of performance management logic.
containment_risks: Risk: If the Performance Management Service is implemented as a monolithic component within NGHE Solution, it may become difficult to scale or evolve independently; mitigation: design the service as a microservice or plugin architecture to enable independent deployment and scaling.

Risk: If worksite context input (Tasking interface) is not well-defined, the Performance Management Service may receive incomplete or inconsistent data, leading to invalid derived targets; mitigation: implement strict validation and require Site Manager approval before baseline commitment.

Risk: If performance derivation algorithms are proprietary or domain-specific, they may be difficult to maintain or transfer to other teams; mitigation: document algorithms clearly, use standard modeling approaches (e.g., regression, lookup tables), and provide training to maintenance teams.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: IM assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) Define the internal architecture of the Performance Management Service (e.g., microservice, module, or component); (2) Specify the Tasking interface contract (worksite context schema, validation rules); (3) Document the performance derivation algorithm specification; (4) Define the baseline database schema and versioning strategy; (5) Specify the reporting and monitoring interface for stakeholders.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Management Service) directly addresses all sub-FRs: (1) ingests worksite context via API (CR-NGHE-005.1), (2) applies derivation algorithms to produce quantitative targets (CR-NGHE-005.2), (3) maintains versioned baseline database (CR-NGHE-005.3), (4) records calibration/update events with metadata (CR-NGHE-005.4), (5) enables performance monitoring and reporting (CR-NGHE-005.5). The DP is logically sound and feasible. No functional gaps identified.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: DP-A is assigned to NGHE Solution, which is the appropriate system-level container. The Performance Management Service is logically cohesive and does not require distribution across multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 95% containment score for DP-A. The service integrates with Vehicle (telemetry input), Teleoperation (operational context), and Safety Services (safety alerts) via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). No significant containment risks identified. The assignment is architecturally sound.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via: (1) functional testing of worksite context capture and storage; (2) validation of performance derivation algorithms against known test cases; (3) inspection of baseline history and calibration records; (4) performance monitoring reports comparing actual vs. derived targets; (5) stakeholder review of accessibility and usability of performance management interface. All verification methods are practical and implementable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear and unambiguous. Sub-FRs are allocated to logical components within NGHE Solution (Performance Management Service, Database, Reporting Interface). Interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are well-defined. Ownership and responsibility are clear.

recommended_revisions: Clarify the performance derivation algorithm specification (e.g., lookup tables, regression models, expert rules, machine learning models) in the next decomposition level.
Define the Tasking interface contract (worksite context schema, validation rules, required vs. optional fields) to ensure consistent input to the Performance Management Service.
Specify the baseline database schema, versioning strategy, and retention policy to ensure traceability and compliance with regulatory requirements.
Document the performance monitoring and reporting interface, including stakeholder access controls, report formats, and alert thresholds.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that must be individually addressed in the next decomposition level. DP-A (Centralized Performance Management Service) is sound and passes the FR-DP gate, but its realization requires detailed design of the worksite context capture mechanism, performance derivation algorithms, baseline database, and reporting interface. IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate and passes the DP-IM gate. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will enable detailed specification of the Performance Management Service architecture and interfaces.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR is complex and multi-faceted, involving data capture, algorithmic derivation, database management, and reporting. Each sub-FR addresses a distinct functional concern and requires independent design and verification. DP-A provides a sound logical solution (centralized service), but the implementation details (algorithms, database schema, interfaces) must be specified in the next level. IM assignment to NGHE Solution is correct, but the internal architecture of the Performance Management Service (microservice, module, component) must be defined in the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Capture and store worksite mission context (site conditions, operational objectives, safety constraints, equipment configuration).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive quantitative performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context using defined algorithms or decision logic.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record and maintain a traceable baseline of performance targets with version control, timestamps, and change rationale.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Manage calibration and update events, documenting the reason, date, and authorized personnel for each change.
CR-NGHE-005.5: Enable performance monitoring and reporting against derived targets with documented evidence of compliance or deviation.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available and can be reliably captured at system initialization and during operation.
Performance derivation algorithms are well-defined and can be implemented deterministically or with documented uncertainty bounds.
Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality personnel have access to Network Link for performance management interface.
Safety Services can receive and process SafetyAlert messages related to performance deviations.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can provide telemetry data (actual cycle time, energy consumption, availability metrics) via Telemetry interface.
open_risks: Risk: Worksite context may be incomplete or inconsistent, leading to invalid derived targets; mitigation: implement validation rules and require Site Manager approval.
Risk: Performance derivation algorithms may require domain expertise that is difficult to encode; mitigation: design for human-in-the-loop review and approval.
Risk: Centralized Performance Management Service may become a single point of failure; mitigation: implement redundancy and fallback mechanisms.
Risk: Performance monitoring may reveal deviations that require rapid response; mitigation: integrate with Safety Services for real-time alerting.

Risk: Regulatory or safety standards may impose specific requirements on performance baseline documentation and traceability; mitigation: engage Safety Manager and Maintenance/Quality early in design.
evidence_needed: Worksite context schema and validation rules (from Site Manager and domain experts).
Performance derivation algorithm specification (from domain experts, historical data analysis, or machine learning models).
Baseline database schema and versioning strategy (from database architects and compliance experts).
Performance monitoring and reporting interface design (from stakeholder interviews with Site Manager, Maintenance/Quality, Safety Manager).
Acceptance criteria for performance target accuracy and traceability (from Safety Manager and Project Manager).
Regulatory or safety standards applicable to performance requirement documentation (from Safety Manager and compliance team).
